title: SubagentHandoff
description: Structured payload every research subagent returns to the coordinator. Each handoff contains a subagent id, a coverage summary describing what was investigated, and a list of claims with explicit per-claim provenance (source URL + confidence).

subagent_id.description: Stable identifier for this subagent within the run (e.g. 'subagent-1').

sub_question.description: The focused sub-question this subagent was dispatched to answer.

coverage.description: One-paragraph description of what the subagent actually managed to investigate. Used by the coordinator to detect coverage gaps.

claims.description: List of claims produced by this subagent. May be empty if the subagent could not produce reliable findings; in that case coverage should explain why.

text.description: The claim itself — a single self-contained factual statement.

source_url.description: URL of the source that supports the claim. May be 'unknown' when the subagent could not attribute a source.

confidence.description: Subagent's self-reported confidence in the claim, between 0 and 1.

entity.description: Optional canonical entity the claim is about (e.g. 'Claude Sonnet 4.5 SWE-bench'). Used by the coordinator to detect cross-subagent conflicts.

error.description: If the subagent failed before producing a full handoff, a short error message. The subagent should still emit coverage + an empty claims array.
